mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in >65% of rate hike periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad consumer demand contraction that depresses corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a negative return differential compared to baseline periods, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in quarterly regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side bottlenecks that trigger short-term risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day returns in >55% of instances during post-shock periods, or the predictive power vanishes outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 325.0, inflation expectations force markets to price in prolonged restrictive policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for Fed forward guidance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate creates a tight labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in quarterly panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with stronger corporate profit forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets price in improved earnings trajectories.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with quarterly GDP revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of falling federal funds rates confirms a soft landing scenario, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns in >55% of easing-cycle instances, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance when Fed rates are stable or rising.
